prompt:
A researcher claims that wetland bird species in the reserve increased steadily from 2018 to 2023.

Which statement is best supported by the data in the table?

choices.text: The value increased by more than 30 species in a single year.
choices.rationale: The largest one-year change was only 20 species.

choices.text: The value changed by the same amount every year, a constant 14 species per year.
choices.rationale: The year-to-year changes vary, so the change was not a constant 14 species.

choices.text: From 2018 to 2023, the value increased every year, going from 188 to 258 species.
choices.rationale: Correct. This matches the steady, one-direction trend the claim describes across every year shown.

choices.text: Almost all of the change happened between 2018 and 2019, with little movement afterward.
choices.rationale: The value changes in every year shown, not just the first span.

explanation: The supporting statement must reflect the table's actual, consistent trend across all the years shown.

table.caption: Wetland bird species in the reserve (species)
